Title: Maize β-amylase7 encodes 2 proteins using alternative transcriptional start sites: Nuclear BAM7 and plastidic BAM2

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream hydrolase step in starch catabolism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for degradation of starch to maltose-producing products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes α-1,4-glucosidic linkages at the non-reducing ends of starch/glucan chains to release maltose
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH14 beta-amylase domain; BAM2-like catalytic domain
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Encodes a plastid-localized catalytically active BAM2-like starch hydrolase
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Produced from the maize BAM7 locus by an alternative downstream transcription start site and shorter transcript; previously shown to be tetrameric and to have sigmoidal kinetics like AtBAM2, but lower catalytic activity than AtBAM2; punctate subchloroplast localization observed
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in maize leaf tissue; RNA-seq/JBrowse indicates expression in endosperm 16 d after pollination, shoots 8 d after sowing, and other tissues; often stronger than the long transcript
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major carbohydrate reserve and transient photosynthate storage in plants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 5′-RACE identifying a shorter transcript; EST/full-length cDNA support; GFP fusion localization in stable transgenic Arabidopsis protoplasts; prior recombinant enzyme assay in E. coli showing catalytic activity, tetramerization, and sigmoidal kinetics
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion or overexpression of the dual-function BAM7 locus may affect both BAM7-like and BAM2-like products
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highlights need to account for hidden dual-function gene architecture when editing or manipulating starch metabolism genes
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq intron-spanning read analysis from NCBI gene pages; CAGE data viewed in UCSC genome browser; tissue expression RNA-seq from maizegdb JBrowse; accession numbers NA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmBAM7-S is generated from an alternative downstream transcriptional start site within the ZmBAM7 locus. 2. The encoded shorter protein contains a predicted chloroplast transit peptide and GFP assays showed plastid localization. 3. Previous functional assays cited in this paper showed ZmBAM7-S is a catalytically active BAM2-like enzyme with tetrameric, sigmoidal properties, supporting a role in starch degradation.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BZR1-like DNA-binding domain fused to a BAM domain; bipartite nuclear localization signal
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Encodes a nuclear-localized BAM7-like protein produced from the upstream transcript of the maize BAM7 locus
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper infers BAM7-like transcription factor function from sequence, domain structure, and localization, but direct target genes and metabolic effects were not experimentally established here
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulating the shared BAM7 locus may simultaneously alter the plastidic BAM2-like product
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq intron-spanning read analysis from NCBI gene pages; CAGE data viewed in UCSC genome browser; tissue expression RNA-seq from maizegdb JBrowse; accession numbers NA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Gene editing or transgene design should distinguish the long nuclear transcript from the short plastidic transcript because both arise from one locus
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in maize leaf tissue; RNA-seq/JBrowse indicates expression in ear primordium 2–4 mm from tip, shoots 8 d after sowing, and other tissues
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 5′-RACE identifying the long transcript; EST/full-length cDNA support; stable GFP localization assay in Arabidopsis protoplasts using the DNA-binding domain containing the NLS
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmBAM7-L is produced from an upstream transcriptional start site of the maize BAM7 locus. 2. Its N-terminal BZR1-like DNA-binding domain contains a functional nuclear localization signal confirmed by GFP localization. 3. It is proposed to act as a BAM7-like transcription factor, but its direct metabolic targets were not established in this study.
